title: Today's AI Brief: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
summary: Exploring the latest AI demos and small business applications
imageAlt: Image from source article for 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
imageCaption: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
imageSourceTitle: 9 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI Brief. We're looking at some fresh demos from Google's AI team and exploring how small businesses can leverage AI. Let's dive in.

Google has released 9 demos of their Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 models in action. These demos show how Gemini's ability to reason and create can be applied in various scenarios. I'm looking at the videos, and what stands out to me is the potential for these models to streamline tasks and improve productivity.

One demo that caught my eye is the use of Gemini Omni for content creation. It appears to be able to generate high-quality content, like blog posts and social media updates, with minimal input. Another demo shows Gemini 3.5 being used for data analysis, which could be a game-changer for businesses drowning in data. I'm also intrigued by the potential for Gemini to help with coding and problem-solving.

The MIT Technology Review has an article on how small businesses can leverage AI. It's clear that AI is no longer just for big companies. The article highlights the potential for AI to help small businesses with tasks like accounting, design, and market research. My read is that we're seeing a platform shift in AI adoption, with more focus on making AI accessible to smaller players. This could level the playing field and help small businesses compete more effectively.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
